_comment: Adversarial-review panel composition, as data. The driver (run-review.ps1 / run_review.py) reads this to know which reviewers to run, with which wrapper, model, and reasoning effort. Swap or add reviewers HERE, not in code. INVARIANT: the active (enabled) reviewer set must span at least `minVendors` distinct vendors — uncorrelated error across vendors is the entire point of the panel. A panel of same-vendor reviewers is just self-review.

wrappers._comment: Each wrapper is a sibling .ps1 with a uniform contract: -Instruction -DiffPath [-FindingsPath] [-ContextPath...] -Model, returning the review text on stdout and a non-zero exit on failure. `effort` and `repoAccess` are honoured only by wrappers that declare the matching parameter (-Effort / -RepoPath); the driver introspects each wrapper and silently omits a flag the wrapper does not support. Today only claude-review.ps1 exposes both — Copilot and Gemini CLIs do not surface a clean reasoning-effort flag, so `effort` is a no-op for them.

alternates._comment: Not in the default panel. Enable to add a fourth vendor or to replace a reviewer above. Do NOT pair a Gemini reviewer with a Gemini-backed host (e.g. an Antigravity/`agy` adjudication) acting as judge over the same finding — same-vendor reviewer-and-judge re-correlates the error the panel exists to decorrelate.
alternates.label: Gemini

roles._comment: Judgment roles the host runs after the driver's blind+cross-examine spine. Under Claude Code these may instead run as in-process Agent-tool calls; the driver and SKILL.md describe both paths. effort/repoAccess apply as above.
